body.title: Router: migrate all model-pinning callers onto domain-routing (evaluator keeps pin_reason)
body.description:
**Problem:** ~10 production devices pin a specific model on InferenceRequest today (grep found reader/node_extractor, reader/device, intent, summarizer, critic, evaluator, minion/tool_loop, librarian/research, scraps, igor/cognition/{multi_cloud,relay,inference_gateway}). Under the domain-routing intention, pinning is only sanctioned for 3 uses (inference-testing, Akien's experiments, model-competition); everything else must route by {domain, urgency}. Nothing is running (verified 2026-07-01: no UU processes, CC.0/CC.1 flags .false), so this is a safe static migration, not a flag-day. **Proposed shape:** GREP the current full list of non-empty model= pinners at build time (do NOT trust this stale enumeration). For each pinner, classify: (a) SANCTIONED (evaluator model_eval_run = model_competition; evaluator/critic judge model if used to test the inference system) -> keep the pin but attach the sanctioned pin_reason (added in T-inference-pin-gate-enforce; this ticket may land the pin_reason FIELD); (b) PRODUCTION (reader extract, intent predict, summarizer, minion, librarian research, scraps, igor cognition) -> REMOVE the pin, set domain (coding where it's a coding task, else generalist='') + the appropriate difficulty tier, let the router pick cheapest-capable. Behavior WILL change (cheapest-capable replaces the pinned model) — that is the intended effect; the per-call cost/outcome record (T-inference-cost-learn-verify) is how each is validated on live tickets. **Affected files:** all pinning callers enumerated above (per-device edits). **Design rules:** NO SQLITE; ALL inference through the Proxy; log the routing decision at each crossing; shim.py design-center field additions are optional-with-defaults. **Scope boundary:** IN: remove production pins -> domain+tier; tag sanctioned pins for pin_reason. OUT: the reject-enforcement flip (T-inference-pin-gate-enforce, gated after this); adding new domains beyond coding/generalist. **Test plan (stub-first red->green):** (1) grep shows ZERO unsanctioned non-empty model= pins in production device code after migration; (2) each migrated device dispatches with a domain (coding or '') and no model pin; (3) evaluator's model_eval_run still pins per-model (competition) and is tagged sanctioned; (4) existing per-device tests still pass (or are updated to assert domain routing). **Completion criteria:** every production pinner routes by domain; only sanctioned pins remain, each tagged with a pin_reason; tests green.

## Pre-computed constraints (auto-stamped at add time)
[hard_block] ⛔ NO SQLITE. EVER. POSTGRES OR FLAT-FILE ONLY. See banner at top of this file. — applies: all (source: CLAUDE.md#hard-rules)
[hard_block] Log every state change and every interface crossing. State changes: ticket status — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] No TheIgors imports. UnseenUniversity must be portable without TheIgors present. — applies: all (source: CLAUDE.md#hard-rules)
[error] No live keys or passwords in source. `.env` is gitignored. — applies: all (source: CLAUDE.md#hard-rules)
[error] `pip install -e .` must succeed at all times (even with empty stubs). — applies: all (source: CLAUDE.md#hard-rules)
[error] Canonical dev-process memory — one home, no exceptions. Every dev-process artifact — — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] device.py and shim.py are the design center. Every component inherits from — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] bus/ owns comms:// routing. Nothing outside bus/ speaks to IMAP directly. — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] skeleton/ owns the MCP aggregator and flat-file registry. No Postgres dependency. — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] devices/ contains one subdirectory per device; each is independently deployable. — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] `~/.unseen_university/logs/<device>/<stream>/` is the canonical log hierarchy — — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] Every ticket closes proven, or declares itself unproven — no discriminator. A ticket that — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
…and 39 more (lower-severity or capped) — query constraints_get for the full set
body.result: 6 production pinners migrated to {domain,task_class} routing (intent/scraps→minion; reader/node-extract/summarizer→worker; critic/evaluator→analyst), 2 sanctioned pins tagged (model_competition, inference_test), latent default model pin ('openai/gpt-4o-mini'→'') removed, dead constants + per-device model env-vars removed, pin_reason field + SANCTIONED_PIN_REASONS added. Advisor-confirmed: pinned strings weren't in registry so all 6 already routed worker-tier — assigned task_class IS the deliberate behavior change. 3 new tests green (zero unsanctioned InferenceRequest pins via balanced-paren scan); 0 regressions (21 pre-existing failures identical on clean HEAD). Filed T-librarian-inference-onto-proxy for 3 discovered librarian Proxy bypasses. Commit 258168b8.
body.intention: I intend that every production caller routes by domain and only the three sanctioned uses pin a model, so 'cheapest capable every time' holds across the whole rack, not just DS.
body.unproven_reason: Two levers: (1) the static criterion (zero unsanctioned pins) IS an authentic assertion, but proof_emitter's diff-inversion strips the new test file (added-file → ImportError collateral, not assertion-red) — needs a proof mode that runs a repo-invariant test at HEAD without a failing pre-state; (2) the BEHAVIORAL correctness (cheapest-capable actually serves each migrated task acceptably) is validated per the ticket's own plan on LIVE tickets via the per-call cost/outcome record (T-inference-cost-learn-verify), which isn't wired/run yet — that's the real proof and it's the next ticket.
